entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: Complete assigned models, focusing on craftsmanship. Students work with cardboard to create 3-dimensional geometric shapes.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content:
Grade 5 students who are new to sloyd should begin with Sloyd: Level 1 and then continue courses in sequence.

Prerequisite: Sloyd: Level 3

Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Sloyd: Level 4
☐ View the "Sloyd Introduction Video" for an overview of materials and resources used in Sloyd (see Quick Links)

☐ Allow time for loading and viewing videos. We recommend teachers preview tutorials and work a model before teaching it.

☐ Refresh students on how to use a ruler (note where "0" starts) and how to use the edge to draw a straight line.

☐ Purchase supplies and introduce students to their Sloyd tools (discuss their care and safe use). Organize them so they are easily accessible.

blocks.text: Lessons will go much more smoothly if you prepare thoroughly. As students gain experience, develop fine motor skills, and practice habits of precision they will need less support.
Watch video tutorials or make the model yourself. Gather supplies and materials needed.
Connect back to previous lesson and skills learned.
Read the instructions one step at a time. Model each step and make sure students complete it with precision before moving to the next step.
When a project is complete, students may repeat it as many times as they wish to give as gifts or create original patterns similar to the project.

lessons.title: 30m Sloyd: Level 4 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Paper Work Model 1 Wall Pocket
⍞ Materials: Sloyd by Schwartz, tagboard, ruler, pencil, scissors, tape, awl, hole punch, thread, needle (optional)

➜ PREP
Before the term begins, read p.3-9 to get an understanding of and a refresher on the practice and method of sloyd. It is good to be reminded of the importance of the instructional words you use to help your students understand and focus. Reading the introduction will also allow you to become familiar with the terminology in a new book before teaching your student. 

➜ VIEW
Photo of the model and tutorial. Visualize the steps to work the model.
∞ Image & Video Link: Wall Pocket

➜ PRACTICE
Work on the model. Read and demonstrate instructions one step at a time.
Paper Work: 3-D Geometric Shapes "No.1: Wall Pocket" p.10
Tack the wall pocket to your bulletin board or message board as a way to organize small things like thumbtacks, paper clips, flower clippings, etc.

Use it as a gift box, or stuff it with a small note to give to a friend or family member. It can also be used to hold and organize small items, such as thumbtacks, paperclips, etc.
